summary: Unmerged Claude-generated batch PR that attempted to mark ftCrazyHand init as matching by implementing fn_80157080 and flipping ftCh_Init.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The diff also included two newly decompiled grKinokoRoute stage functions, but the PR was closed quickly because the worktree commits had build issues that needed to be fixed before batching.
what_changed: In configure.py, changed Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c").

Replaced the nonmatching marker for fn_80157080 in ftCh_Init.c with a small implementation that calls Fighter_ChangeMotionState with motion state 0x158, three ftCh_Init_804DA070/804DA074 float arguments, NULL, then calls ftAnim_8006EBA4.
Added an extern declaration for float grNKr_804DB830 in grkinokoroute.c.

Implemented grKinokoRoute_80207490 to get a ground-related gobj via Ground_801C57A4, read a Vec3 position with ftLib_80086644, call Ground_801C38BC(pos.x, grNKr_804DB830 + pos.y), and update the camera with Camera_8002F3AC when the gobj is non-NULL.
Implemented grKinokoRoute_80207ADC to get the Ground struct from a Ground_GObj, call grAnime_801C8138(gobj, gp->map_id, 0), and set gp->x11_flags.b012 = 2.
PR body claimed fn_80157080 matched 100%, but the PR was closed unmerged due to build issues in the worktree commits.
smart_moves: The ftCrazyHand implementation is minimal and uses existing engine helpers, suggesting the intended match was achieved by expressing the function as a straightforward motion-state transition plus animation callback rather than introducing local control flow.
The grKinokoRoute_80207490 implementation preserves an explicit NULL check before dereferencing or using the object returned by Ground_801C57A4.
The stage code uses existing typed access patterns such as GET_GROUND(gobj) and named bitfield gp->x11_flags.b012, which is preferable to raw pointer arithmetic when available.
The configure.py flip to Matching was correctly scoped to the ftCrazyHand source file, but this was only appropriate if all functions in that object built and matched.
decomp_lessons: Do not batch unrelated matching work unless every touched object builds cleanly; this PR mixed ftCrazyHand and grKinokoRoute changes and was closed for source build issues.
Before switching an object from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, verify the entire object, not only the targeted function, because a single source or declaration issue can block the batch.

Small boss-character state transition functions can often match as a direct Fighter_ChangeMotionState call followed by ftAnim_8006EBA4, but exact constants and float argument globals must be preserved.
For stage decompilation, prefer existing ground and camera helper APIs and typed Ground accessors when reconstructing short behavior functions.

Extern declarations such as grNKr_804DB830 should be validated against headers or symbol ownership; adding file-local externs can unblock compilation but may indicate missing or incomplete declarations elsewhere.
A claimed 100% match is not enough for acceptance if CI/build verification is absent or failing.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-based names such as fn_80157080, grKinokoRoute_80207490, and grKinokoRoute_80207ADC.
Crazy Hand functions follow the ftCh_ prefix, as seen in ftCh_Entry_Phys, ftCh_Entry_Coll, and ftCh_Damage_Anim.
Kinoko Route stage functions use the grKinokoRoute_ prefix.
External data symbols preserve address-based names such as grNKr_804DB830.
Engine helpers retain subsystem prefixes such as Fighter_, ftAnim_, Ground_, Camera_, ftLib_, and grAnime_.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a direct void function body for a previously nonmatching marker when the target assembly is just a helper-call sequence.
For motion-state transitions, preserve literal state IDs and argument order exactly in Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Reuse global float constants or data symbols directly when they appear in arithmetic, as with grNKr_804DB830 + pos.y.
Keep NULL checks structured simply around helper calls to avoid altering control flow shape.
Use typed local variables such as Vec3 pos, HSD_GObj* gobj, and Ground* gp to express stack and struct usage clearly.

review_feedback.feedback: Closing - worktree commits have build issues that need to be fixed at the source before batching.
review_feedback.implication: The PR was not merged; future reuse should first reproduce and fix build errors before adopting these implementations or configure.py changes.

follow_up_queries: Check whether fn_80157080 was later matched and merged in another PR, and compare the final accepted implementation against this attempted version.
Search for later fixes to ftCh_Init.c build issues before reusing the configure.py Matching flip.
Search for grKinokoRoute_80207490 and grKinokoRoute_80207ADC in later merged PRs to determine whether these stage implementations were correct or needed adjustment.
Investigate whether grNKr_804DB830 should be declared in a header instead of as a local extern in grkinokoroute.c.
Review CI or local build logs for the closed worktree batch if available to identify the exact source-level build failure.
